rules.title: Axios compromised npm releases with embedded RAT
rules.summary: Compromised axios versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 contained an obfuscated dropper (setup.js in plain-crypto-js) that downloaded and executed a platform-specific remote access trojan from sfrclak.com. The dropper self-deletes and replaces its package.json with a clean stub after execution.

dropper_packages.notes: Dropper published as v4.2.1 (v4.2.0 is a decoy). Contains setup.js which downloads the RAT from sfrclak.com:8000. The dropper self-deletes and replaces its package.json with a clean stub, but the directory presence confirms it ran.

host_indicators.notes: RAT binary for macOS, disguised as a system daemon. Presence confirms active compromise.
RAT binary for Windows. Presence confirms active compromise.
VBScript loader used to execute the PowerShell stage. Part of the Windows infection chain.
PowerShell payload that downloads and installs the Windows RAT binary.
RAT payload for Linux. Presence confirms active compromise.
C2 server used to download RAT payloads. Endpoint: http://sfrclak.com:8000/6202033
C2 server IP address for sfrclak.com.

remediation.summary: Pin axios to a safe version, remove the dropper, regenerate lockfiles, and escalate immediately if host indicators were found.
steps: Pin axios to a known-good version (e.g., 1.14.0 or latest safe release).
Remove plain-crypto-js from node_modules if present.
Clear npm cache: npm cache clean --force
If gouvernante reported host indicator findings: STOP. The machine is compromised. Isolate it immediately, rotate ALL credentials, tokens, and SSH keys, and follow your incident response procedure.
Re-run gouvernante to verify a clean scan (exit code 0).
Report status (clean or compromised) to your team lead.
